response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile engineer with genuine IDE/tooling exposure (C/C++ work on National Instruments' LabWindows/CVI IDE) and excellent software-craftsmanship and testing credentials. However, the role's core requirements—deep JVM understanding, strong Java AND Kotlin, and solid Spring/enterprise-framework experience—are largely absent. Java appears only peripherally (robotic arms, device orchestration), there is no Kotlin or Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience, and the bulk of the career is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. The stack mismatch on the central qualifications outweighs the relevant IDE and tooling background.
strengths: Direct IDE-internals experience building C/C++ components for the LabWindows/CVI IDE
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus testing tooling authorship
Broad systems experience including low-latency, high-throughput microservices and developer tooling
concerns: No Kotlin and only shallow, peripheral Java experience; no demonstrated deep JVM expertise
No Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate or enterprise-framework experience required by the role
Recent and primary work is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the JVM ecosystem
